text.title: REST Resource Design Rules
text.summary: URL naming conventions, HTTP method semantics, and query parameter patterns.

rule.statement: Use plural nouns for resource collections: /users, /orders, /products.
rule.rationale: Plural names make the collection vs. singleton distinction consistent: /users (collection) vs /users/{id} (singleton).

rule.statement: Use lowercase with hyphens: /user-profiles, not /UserProfiles or /user_profiles.
rule.rationale: URLs are case-sensitive on most servers. Hyphens are readable in links; underscores can be hidden by text underline rendering.

rule.statement: Use verbs only for actions that do not map to CRUD: /payments/{id}/refund, /users/{id}/activate.
rule.rationale: Pure noun-based URLs cover 90% of cases. Actions on resources are the accepted exception.

rule.statement: Nest sub-resources to express ownership: /users/{id}/orders for a user's orders.
rule.rationale: Nesting makes the ownership relationship explicit in the URL. Limit nesting to two levels to avoid unwieldy paths.

rule.statement: Use query parameters for filtering, sorting, and pagination: ?status=active&sort=-created_at&limit=20.
rule.rationale: Query parameters do not alter resource identity. They allow the same resource URL to serve filtered views.

rule.statement: GET must be safe and idempotent; POST creates and is neither; PUT replaces (idempotent); PATCH updates fields (not idempotent); DELETE removes (idempotent).
rule.rationale: Clients and proxies rely on method semantics for caching (GET), retry safety (idempotent), and optimistic UI updates.

rule.statement: Return 201 Created with a Location header and the created resource in the body for successful POST.
rule.rationale: Location lets clients navigate to the new resource without a second request. Body avoids a follow-up GET.

rule.statement: Return 204 No Content for successful DELETE with no response body.
rule.rationale: A deleted resource has nothing to return. Returning 200 with an empty body is ambiguous.
